senses.definition: plural of catechist: people who teach the principles of a religion, especially Christianity, to others.
examples: The catechists led the Sunday class for new converts.
Several catechists volunteered to accompany the students on the church retreat.

senses.definition: capable of being catechized; able to be taught the basic principles of a religion.
examples: The material was designed to be catechizable for junior students.
In many mission schools, all children are considered catechizable.

senses.definition: the act or process of teaching the basic principles of a religion; formal instruction in doctrine.
examples: The catechization of new believers took place over several weeks.
Catechization is often done in small groups to ensure understanding.

senses.definition: plural of catechization; multiple instances or programs of religious instruction.
examples: The diocese offered several catechizations this spring.
Historically, catechizations varied in length across communities.

senses.definition: to teach the basic beliefs of a religion, usually by instruction and questioning.
examples: The priest catechizes the new converts.
Teachers in Sunday school catechize children about faith and morals.

senses.definition: past tense of catechize; taught the basic beliefs of a religion.
examples: He catechized the candidates before baptism.
She was catechized as a child in the old parish.

senses.definition: a person who catechizes others; a religious teacher.
examples: The catechizer led the class with clear questions.
As a catechizer, he was patient and articulate.

senses.definition: plural of catechizer; multiple people who catechize.
examples: The diocese recruited several catechizers for the program.
Different catechizers used varied teaching methods.

senses.definition: third person singular of catechize; teaches the basic beliefs of a religion.
examples: She catechizes new members every Sunday.
The program catechizes youth through discussion and storytelling.

senses.definition: present participle of catechize; the act of teaching religious principles.
examples: Catechizing the class requires patience and clarity.
They spent the afternoon catechizing the group about faith.

senses.definition: plural form of catechizing; occurrences or instances of religious instruction. Note: this plural is rare; more common is 'catechizings' used in some texts.
examples: Different catechizings occurred across the parishes.
Several catechizings were part of the curriculum.

senses.definition: a chemical structure (a benzene ring with two adjacent hydroxyl groups) found in some natural compounds and used in chemistry.
examples: Catechol is a common motif in aromatic chemistry.
The compound contains a catechol moiety.

senses.definition: a type of hormone and neurotransmitter such as dopamine, epinephrine, or norepinephrine.
examples: Catecholamines play key roles in stress responses.
Dopamine is a well-known catecholamine in the brain.

senses.definition: of or relating to catecholamines or their action as neurotransmitters or hormones.
examples: catecholaminergic neurons show dopamine signaling.
The study focuses on catecholaminergic pathways.

senses.definition: plural of catecholamine; hormones/neurotransmitters such as dopamine, epinephrine, and norepinephrine.
examples: Catecholamines are released during stress.
Levels of catecholamines can affect heart rate.

senses.definition: organic compounds with a benzene ring bearing two adjacent hydroxyl groups; related to catechol.
examples: Catechols are important intermediates in organic synthesis.
The compound contains several catechols.

senses.definition: a dark astringent extract from the wood of the acacia tree, used in traditional medicines and as a tanning agent.
examples: Catechu is used in traditional remedies.
The ink was prepared with catechu extracts.

senses.definition: a person who is being instructed in the Christian faith before baptism; an inquirer becoming a Christian.
examples: The catechumen attended classes before baptism.
Old church records mention many catechumens in the early centuries.

senses.definition: relating to catechumens or the catechumenate stage in Christian initiation.
examples: catechumenal rites are performed before baptism.
The catechumenal journey includes instruction and sponsorship.

senses.definition: the period of instruction and formation for catechumens before baptism; the catechetical process.
examples: The catechumenate lasted several months.
During the catechumenate, candidates study doctrine and participate in worship.

senses.definition: plural of catechumenate; multiple instruction periods before baptism.
examples: Several catechumenates were scheduled this year.
The diocese supported catechumenates across parishes.

senses.definition: relating to catechumens or to catechesis and early Christian instruction.
examples: catechumenical writings guided early converts.
The rite has catechumenical significance.

senses.definition: in a way that relates to catechumens or catechetical instruction.
examples: The group studied catechumenically, focusing on core tenets.
They organized catechumenically structured sessions.

senses.definition: the system or doctrine of catechesis and instructions for new Christians; the practice of catechizing.
examples: Catechumenism has shaped many Christian communities.
Scholars debate aspects of early catechumenism.

senses.definition: plural of catechumenism; multiple systems or instances of catechetical instruction.
examples: The conference compared various catechumenisms.
Different communities practiced distinct catechumenisms.
